title: Post-stroke follow-up

medication.text: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.
medication.rationale: Supports multidisciplinary stroke assessment, goals and rehabilitation plan for post-stroke follow-up; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.14 — stroke assessment and rehabilitation plan

follow_up.text: Supports post-transfer follow-up, information needs, daily activities, participation goals, work, driving and transport discussion, and review of health and social care needs at 6 months and annually.
follow_up.rationale: Supports transfer and long-term review for post-stroke follow-up; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports post-transfer follow-up, information needs, daily activities, participation goals, work, driving and transport discussion, and review of health and social care needs at 6 months and annually.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.17, 1.4.3, and 1.17.1–1.17.5 — transfer and long-term review

additions.text: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.
additions.rationale: Supports multidisciplinary stroke assessment, goals and rehabilitation plan for post-stroke follow-up; no decision is generated.
additions.evidence_extract: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Stroke date and type, deficits, function, swallowing, cognition, mood, medicines, rehabilitation, complications, goals and specialist plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for post-stroke follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
